title: Random Question Are the EVA Movies rare
display_title: Random Question Are the EVA Movies rare
wikitext:
[]: Random Question - Are the EVA Movies rare?             Quote Post by AntaBaka! » Wed Dec 09, 2009 1:55 am Sorry, I'm not sure if this is the right place, and it is also my 2nd post. So... to the point. When looking on Ebay, particularly my country's one, I've found it hard to stumble across the 2 EVA movies. I know they're out of print, but are they really rare? I am asking this because I'd like to sell mine, because one copy of EoE was sold for around $115, it was used also. If the price went for that much for me, I'd definitely sell it. (I may get more cash in the future, or it may not be worth it, but I've hardly watched it, and I don't think I'll ever understand it.) I'm asking if it is rare or not because if it is, I think should sell it at a reasonable 'rare' price. And if it isn't, I'd look like a fool. *Mind you this is a R4 (Aust.) release, which is slightly different to the other releases, and is / was published by Madman, which no longer holds the license for the EVA movies. So I'm just asking for your opinion guys, I may sound like a fool, but that's for you to decide. Anyway thanks to anyone who reads. PS: EDITED

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Wed Dec 09, 2009 2:53 am Manga recently lost the rights to the films, not Madman. Madman is just the Australian distributer. Also, are you AsukaShin from the Madman forums?

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Wed Dec 09, 2009 3:06 am EGF isn't the place to try to sell your DVDs. If I were you I'd at least watch the film once. It's not really something you ever fully "understand" but the wiki and forums can help you put some of the pieces together.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Dec 09, 2009 8:35 am Wow. $115? I got D&R and EoE together off eBay for like $30, if that.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Dec 09, 2009 8:56 am Out of print, but I still see it in stores. My shitty Best Buy has it.

[]:             Quote Post by zilluss » Wed Dec 09, 2009 9:44 am The German version is out of print (though Panini still got the rights I suppose). I had luck with my DVD because I asked a seller on a platform similar to ebay if he sells EoE& D&R to me for 60€'s. You can still get D&R for around 20-30 € but EoE is at 100€ (buy it now)

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Wed Dec 09, 2009 10:28 am heh. I bought EoE used off of amazon for $20, but that ain't stopping me from arranging a fan DVD using the Renewal picture quality

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Dec 09, 2009 11:19 am GasmaskAvenger wrote: heh. I bought EoE used off of amazon for $20, but that ain't stopping me from arranging a fan DVD using the Renewal picture quality

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Wed Dec 09, 2009 12:36 pm "Value" is in the eye of the buyer - if someone will buy it for $100, that's what it's worth. I'm not aware that any particular regions of EoE or D&R are particularly collectible over others - the market force is just whether the buyer has a player/drive that will play that region. When to sell? Before someone releases a new version of what you want to sell. With the release of Rebuild, it seems to me that there will be both renewed interest in the old films as well as eentually new releases of the old films. It can be as much of an art as a science in getting maximum price. Ask anyone with an original edition of the Star Trek Galileo Christmas ornament. Marketplace will also play a factor - if your selling on a national or international auction site, there will be competing items bringing prices down - or, if it really is rare, there will be no other examples and you work the beauty that is an auction - if lots of people wnat it, the price goes up. In the US, I still occasionally see an EoE in a used DVD bin, and there are a couple copies on eBay, $10-28. But that's Region 1. So...I guess check out the eBay prices in your area and Region, decide how much you want to part with it, and search out Websites that have news about new DVD releases. Once it's rereleased, the value will drop.

[]:             Quote Post by zilluss » Wed Dec 09, 2009 2:47 pm Usually you could just go with the US Version as you can import it for way less than 100$. But I think the German dub is really good (of course not better than Japanese but way better than the English dub) and so for some it's worth the money.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Wed Dec 09, 2009 3:25 pm The rarity is temporary; wait for the Blue-Ray release.

[]:             Quote Post by ath » Wed Dec 09, 2009 4:16 pm Allemann wrote: The rarity is temporary; wait for the Blue-Ray release.

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Wed Dec 09, 2009 4:34 pm The DVD's are pretty rare since I got both Rebirth and EoE on Amazon for $50 for both of them.

[]:             Quote Post by Ambrosia » Wed Dec 09, 2009 6:48 pm I bought D&R and EoE for some €35 each years ago, both of them brand new. Back then I found them quite expensive, but as I haven't seem them in physical stores in ages I have no idea how much they would cost over here right now.

[]:             Quote Post by schismatics » Wed Dec 09, 2009 7:57 pm I think they're kinda hard to find brand new. I stumbled upon EoE at a Borders for $25 but I got D&R used for like $5 off amazon.com

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Thu Dec 10, 2009 12:31 am I bought the boxed set used on Amazon for about $25. That was in... May '09.

[]:             Quote Post by Kamina » Wed Dec 16, 2009 8:01 pm Its going to go up in price due to it being temporarily out of print to until another company buys the license, if i was one of the guys with the crappy best buy i would buy the copies and sell them on ebay for 60 bucks to make a few bucks. So long as your price looks like a steal in comparison to others prices it will sell pretty fast. I think i bought mine for 40 at best buy the last one mine had, and i remember they had all of macross plus for like 300 bucks and looked online and saw on ebay it sold for a massive amount, so im guessing my store hiked the price to make more profit off of it sense it also was out of print.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Wed Dec 16, 2009 8:26 pm I say, wait until a new distributer re-releases the movies using the "renewal" quality. (I don't care who gets the new rights, just as long as they give full respect to the movies)
